narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school and then hearing you'll likely struggle with the bar exam feels like a gut punch, especially with your solid 3.6 undergraduate GPA and middle-class family background supporting you all the way. Our prediction model looked at your full picture and saw a 65.9% chance of not passing the bar, painting a story where a few key hurdles stand out strongest. What weighed heaviest was your Year 3 decile of 2, placing you near the bottom of your class then, which signaled to the model a tough climb in the rigorous law school environment ahead. Right behind that, your LSAT score of 28 pulled strongly in the same direction, as it's on the lower side compared to what's typically needed to thrive through bar prep. Your Year 1 decile of 2 echoed this pattern, suggesting those early struggles might carry over rather than fade. Even with full-time dedication ahead and your race and gender in the mix, factors like family income also nudged the odds toward failure, as middle-bracket resources sometimes fall short for the intense bar grind. Your UGPA, while respectable, couldn't quite offset these core academic signals that dominated the story. It's not that you're incapable—far from it—but the model sees this combination as a risky bet for bar success, one we've seen trip up too many promising students before. The good news? You've got time to target those weak spots, maybe retake the LSAT or build stronger habits, turning this into your origin story for comeback. Hang in there; law's worth fighting for if you recalibrate.
